user_prompt: we are making a big change
for unencrypted audio recordings, instead of recording to a custom format file with a `*.rec` extension, instead, we will record to a plain `*.wav` file extension.
we will do some things to make it easy to assemble a stereo 16 bit PCM file in real time even though the mic is optionally on or off and the bluetooth data might be unreliable as well.
first, I've already asked you to add a `queueSilence` to the `AudioFifo`. This is useful for the next change
when writing to the bt2file FIFO, if we know that the mic is off, then we insert silence into mic2file automatically.
when writing to the mic2file FIFO, and we see the bt2file FIFO is significantly behind, then we insert some silence to let it catch up.

when writing to the file, we only write if both FIFO has data so that the data can be mixed into stereo. Since the enqueuing mechanism now attempts to keep both FIFOs at the same level, this should work.
When opening the file, since it is a `*.wav` file in this case, we put a placeholder for the RIFF header. When the file is about to be closed, we put in the correct data into the RIFF header
I've made changes myself, make sure I didn't make mistakes. I've placed comments around the code too, to indicate my intentions. Some documentation have been edited but I might've missed some.
Basically, if `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` is 0, it records to `*.wav` files directly.

Also please make sure that, inside `scripts\thefly_desktop.py`, if the script sees a `*.wav` file instead of a `*.rec` file, it is saved directly to the folder meant for `*.wav` files, skipping the decoding, and if the user asked for it, do the transcription and summarization.
warn me if something is seriously wrong
agent_reply: Implemented the direct `.wav` recording path for `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL == 0`.
What changed:

- [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:40) now writes `.wav` files for sec0, including placeholder RIFF headers on open and final RIFF headers/truncation on close.

- [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:790) now pumps both file FIFOs into stereo PCM: mic left, BT right, with forced stop-drain using silence for a missing side.

- [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:108) now balances the sec0 file FIFOs with silence: exact match when mic is off, catch-up padding when one side falls behind.
- [AudioFifo.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:95) has `queueSilence()` and [AudioFifo.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:306) has `usedSamples()`.

- [thefly_desktop.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py:180) now saves `.wav` files directly into the `wav` folder and skips decoding; downloaded `.wav` files participate in `--transcribe`.
- Updated the recorder/security docs to reflect `.wav` for unencrypted builds and `.rec` for encrypted builds.
Verification passed:
- `python -c "import ast, pathlib; ast.parse(...thefly_desktop.py...)"`
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0`
- `pio run -e thefly_sec1`
- `git diff --check`

I did not find anything seriously wrong after the pass. The one thing still worth watching on device is real timing behavior during long one-sided streams, since that depends on callback cadence and SD write latency, not just compile-time correctness.

user_prompt: ### Task: major change to recorded encrypted file format
The current implementation uses nearly standard `*.wav` files when unencrypted (lowest security level builds), this is easy to stream and play back.

The current implementation uses a custom format that uses fixed sized packets but each packet is variable length internally, and it’s all encrypted. While this packet can also carry metadata and can be extended to carry event data, it is highly unlikely that the advantages of the flexibility is worth it over the disadvantage of it being difficult to play back in the same way, with easily calculatable audio durations and the ability to seek within a file. The file size is smaller but microSD card performance benchmarks shows that we do not need the smaller file size.
So we are going to throw out the whole custom format idea. We are just going to make encrypted `*.wav` files but call them `*.rec` files.
The document `developer-docs\recording-file-format.md` has been updated and is considered current.
A lot of stuff needs to be removed from `lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp` , but the new `*.wav` writing functions only need very small changes for this to work.
There needs to be two static buffers, one for the size of a chunk of plaintext audio, and the second buffer for the encrypted audio, before it gets written to the file.

Remove `AudioFileDecoder.cpp` and the associated header file completely. Don’t bother editing them to make them work. Benchmarks have shown that they are incredibly slow and the user will never use them. But we will offer live playback, which is fast because the bottleneck is actually just microSD card writing.
The sizes of the plaintext chunk and the result encrypted chunks shall be defined in `inc\defs.h` so the code can use them later, include also the encrypted RIFF header size.
Do not yet edit the playback related code, do not edit the python scripts. We’ll do that later
agent_reply: Implemented the encrypted recording format change.
What changed:
- Added shared encrypted WAV chunk sizing in [inc/defs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:12):
`WAV_ENCRYPTED_AUDIO_PLAINTEXT_LENGTH = 1024`, encrypted audio chunk length, and encrypted RIFF header length.

- Reworked [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:90) so security level 0 still writes normal `.wav`, while security level 1+ writes `.rec` as encrypted WAV-shaped chunks.
- Encrypted `.rec` files now write the RIFF header as the first encrypted chunk, then fixed-size encrypted PCM chunks.
- Final partial encrypted audio chunks are padded with silence so chunk math stays fixed.
- Removed the old packet format definitions from `defs.h`.
- Removed `AudioFileDecoder.cpp`, `AudioFileDecoder.h`, and `test_decoderbenchmark.cpp`.
- Removed decoder benchmark references from `all_tests`.
I did not edit playback or Python scripts for this pass. `lib/WavPlayback/WavPlayback.h` is still dirty from the earlier shared `WAV_RIFF_HEADER_LENGTH` change, not from this task.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0 -e thefly_sec1` passed.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec2 -e thefly_sec3` passed.
- Rebuilt `thefly_sec1` again after the final retry-path tweak; passed.
- Search confirms old packet/decoder identifiers are gone from code.
One note: `git diff --check` only complains about trailing whitespace in the already-updated `developer-docs/recording-file-format.md`; I left that doc untouched.
Implemented the encrypted recording format change.
What changed:
- Added shared encrypted WAV chunk sizing in [inc/defs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:12):
`WAV_ENCRYPTED_AUDIO_PLAINTEXT_LENGTH = 1024`, encrypted audio chunk length, and encrypted RIFF header length.

- Reworked [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:90) so security level 0 still writes normal `.wav`, while security level 1+ writes `.rec` as encrypted WAV-shaped chunks.
- Encrypted `.rec` files now write the RIFF header as the first encrypted chunk, then fixed-size encrypted PCM chunks.
- Final partial encrypted audio chunks are padded with silence so chunk math stays fixed.
- Removed the old packet format definitions from `defs.h`.
- Removed `AudioFileDecoder.cpp`, `AudioFileDecoder.h`, and `test_decoderbenchmark.cpp`.
- Removed decoder benchmark references from `all_tests`.
I did not edit playback or Python scripts for this pass. `lib/WavPlayback/WavPlayback.h` is still dirty from the earlier shared `WAV_RIFF_HEADER_LENGTH` change, not from this task.
Verification:
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0 -e thefly_sec1` passed.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec2 -e thefly_sec3` passed.
- Rebuilt `thefly_sec1` again after the final retry-path tweak; passed.
- Search confirms old packet/decoder identifiers are gone from code.
One note: `git diff --check` only complains about trailing whitespace in the already-updated `developer-docs/recording-file-format.md`; I left that doc untouched.

commit_message: removed custom audio format, changed to encrypted wav

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: lib/AudioFileRecorder/readme.md
## My request for Codex:
ignoring the cloud functionality
the current code is considered the truth, find places where documentation is no longer correct
agent_reply: Ignoring cloud-specific docs, I found these stale/non-truthy documentation spots:
**Incorrect Or Stale**

- [lib/Security/security-levels.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/security-levels.md:17) says level 0 records directly to `*.wav`. Current config has `BUILD_USE_MP3_COMPRESSION` enabled in [inc/conf.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:22), so level 0 records to `*.mp3`.

- [lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md:89) says security-level-0 recordings are `.wav` and all encrypted recordings are `*.rec`. Current code uses `.mp3` for unencrypted MP3 builds and `.fly` for encrypted MP3 builds in [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:768).

- [lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Security/security-policies-specifications.md:239) repeats the same stale `.rec`/`.wav` assumption. Current encrypted MP3 recordings are `.fly`, not `.rec`.

- [lib/AudioFifo/readme.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/readme.md:9) says muted means dequeueing returns zeros. Current code also writes zeros while muted during enqueue paths, so mute is not only a dequeue behavior.
**Probably Misleading**

- [lib/AudioFileRecorder/readme.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/readme.md:7) says FIFO data is “mixed together into a stereo stream.” Current code does not mix the two sources together; it interleaves mic as left and host/Bluetooth as right in [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:1821). “Combined into a stereo stream” would be more accurate.

- [developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:56) says current scratch buffers are 512 bytes. Current recorder/audio paths use larger configured buffers for MP3/WAV chunks, so this reads stale, though it is in a sizing discussion rather than an API contract.
**Looks Mostly Still Correct**
- [lib/AudioFileRecorder/readme.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/readme.md:3) is broad but still true: MP3 compression and encryption are supported.

- [developer-docs/recording-file-format.md](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/recording-file-format.md:1) describes both WAV and MP3 modes, so I would not call it wrong, though it could be clearer that MP3 is currently enabled by default in this repo.
